channel.title: Google-Research on Daily Tech Articles Feed
channel.description: Recent content in Google-Research on Daily Tech Articles Feed

item.title: Vibe Coding XR: Accelerating AI + XR prototyping with XR Blocks and Gemini
item.description: 🚀 Vibe Coding XR introduces a new workflow that enhances rapid prototyping for XR applications. By integrating the open-source XR Blocks framework with Gemini Canvas, creators can turn user prompts into interactive WebXR apps for Android headsets swiftly. This innovation aims to simplify the development process in extended reality. Large language models (LLMs) are transforming software engineering, making it easier to test new user interfaces and educational experiences. Catch a live demo at…

item.title: TurboQuant: Redefining AI efficiency with extreme compression
item.description: 🚀 Introducing TurboQuant: a breakthrough in AI efficiency! This new set of quantization algorithms offers substantial compression for large language models and vector search engines. By optimizing high-dimensional vector representation, TurboQuant enhances vector search and reduces memory costs. Traditional methods often face memory overhead, but TurboQuant effectively addresses this, streamlining data processing. Learn more about the future of AI at #ICLR2026! #AI #TurboQuant…

item.title: Mapping the modern world: How S2Vec learns the language of our cities
item.description: Introducing S2Vec, a groundbreaking self-supervised framework that enhances our understanding of geospatial data. 🌍 This innovation transforms complex geographic features into general-purpose embeddings, enabling the prediction of socioeconomic and environmental patterns globally. S2Vec allows AI to recognize neighborhood characteristics, improving predictions on metrics like population density and environmental impact. 📊 While it shows strong performance in socioeconomic tasks, there’s room…

item.title: Google Research at The Check Up: from healthcare innovation to real-world care settings
item.description: 🚀 Exciting developments in AI are transforming healthcare! At The Check Up, Google Research highlighted new innovations aimed at enhancing scientific and clinical research. Collaborating with healthcare providers and experts, they prioritize safety and accuracy in their projects. 🩺 Key breakthroughs include the Personal Health Agent (PHA), which offers personalized care by combining insights from data scientists and health coaches. This integrated approach supports long-term health better…

item.title: Improving breast cancer screening workflows with machine learning
item.description: Enhancing breast cancer screening is crucial as it remains a leading cause of death for women aged 35-64 in the UK. The NHS currently employs a double-read workflow, which faces challenges due to a shortage of clinical radiologists. Recent studies highlight the potential of AI in this process. Collaborating with NHS organizations, the AIMS study explores how AI can improve detection accuracy and reduce workloads for radiologists. Stay informed about advancements in healthcare technology! 🩺🤖…

item.title: Testing LLMs on superconductivity research questions
item.description: Can large language models (LLMs) become expert partners in physics research? A recent study tested six LLMs on high-temperature superconductivity questions. The research, published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, involved expert evaluation of LLM responses. The top performers came from systems using certified sources, like NotebookLM. Findings highlight areas for improvement in LLM capabilities, aiming to enhance their role in scientific discovery. #AIinScience…

item.title: Protecting cities with AI-driven flash flood forecasting
item.description: 🌧️ We’re enhancing urban flood safety with AI-driven flash flood forecasting! This new initiative provides up to 24 hours of advance notice for rapid-onset floods, which account for 85% of flood-related fatalities globally. While developed nations have robust systems, many regions in the Global South lack early warning systems. Our goal is to bridge this gap and protect communities. Learn more about this crucial step in climate resilience! 🌍🌊 #FloodForecasting #ClimateResilience #AI…

item.title: Introducing Groundsource: Turning news reports into data with Gemini
item.description: 🌍 Introducing Groundsource, a new methodology that transforms unstructured news into actionable data. Our first dataset focuses on urban flash floods, featuring 2.6 million records from over 150 countries. This open-access resource aims to enhance forecasting and disaster preparedness. Groundsource addresses the global data scarcity in hydrological hazards, paving the way for improved climate research and community resilience. #ClimateData #FlashFloods #DisasterPreparedness #Groundsource…

item.title: Exploring the feasibility of conversational diagnostic AI in a real-world clinical study
item.description: 🚀 Exciting developments in AI for healthcare! A recent study in collaboration with Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center assessed AMIE, a conversational AI designed for clinical reasoning. This research marks a key step in evaluating its real-world application in primary care settings. The study focused on how AMIE assists in gathering patient information before visits and examines perceptions from both clinicians and patients about using AI in healthcare. The findings contribute to…

item.title: WAXAL: A large-scale open resource for African language speech technology
item.description: WAXAL is a groundbreaking resource for African language speech technology, providing open access to a vast dataset for 27 native languages. This initiative aims to bridge the technology gap for over 100 million speakers in Sub-Saharan Africa, enabling better interaction with AI systems. Developed with local organizations, WAXAL offers 1,846 hours of transcribed speech data for automatic speech recognition. Learn more about this important development! 🌍🎤📊 #WAXAL #SpeechTechnology…

item.title: Where wild things roam: Identifying wildlife with SpeciesNet
item.description: 🚀 SpeciesNet, an AI tool for identifying wildlife in camera trap images, is now widely used after going open-source last year. 🌍 With the ability to classify nearly 2,500 animal categories, it has become essential for conservation efforts globally. 📸 Research groups are utilizing SpeciesNet to identify species like pumas in Colombia and elephants in Tanzania, driving new insights into wildlife patterns. #WildlifeConservation #AI #SpeciesNet #GoogleEarthAI #Sustainability

item.title: Teaching LLMs to reason like Bayesians
item.description: Researchers are teaching large language models (LLMs) to reason using Bayesian methods. This approach involves training LLMs to mimic predictions from an optimal Bayesian model. By doing so, LLMs can better understand user preferences through ongoing interactions. This helps improve personalized recommendations, moving beyond simple assumptions. The findings suggest that LLMs can effectively learn and generalize reasoning skills across various tasks. This method may enhance their performance…

item.title: Teaching AI to read a map
item.description: 🚀 A new system for synthetic data generation aims to train AI to navigate maps effectively. Current multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can recognize images but struggle with spatial reasoning, like tracing paths on maps. The project, “MapTrace,” introduces a dataset and a synthetic data generation pipeline that teaches this essential skill. It includes 2M Q&A pairs for research use. #AI #MachineLearning #DataScience #SpatialReasoning #MapTrace

item.title: Scheduling in a changing world: Maximizing throughput with time-varying capacity
item.description: New algorithms have been developed for scheduling jobs on cloud infrastructure, addressing the challenges posed by fluctuating machine availability. ⚙️ In cloud computing, resource availability varies due to factors like maintenance and hardware failures. This dynamic environment complicates scheduling, especially for non-preemptive tasks that can’t be paused. The research presented in “Non-preemptive Throughput Maximization under Time-varying Capacity” explores maximizing job throughput…

item.title: Beyond one-on-one: Authoring, simulating, and testing dynamic human-AI group conversations
item.description: Introducing DialogLab, a new open-source framework designed for simulating dynamic human-AI group conversations. 🌐🤖 It allows researchers to create and test multi-party dialogues, blending structured scripts with real-time improvisation. This is crucial for capturing the complexity of human interactions in various settings like team meetings and classrooms. Evaluations with experts show that DialogLab supports effective design and iteration for realistic conversational experiences. #AI…

item.title: How AI trained on birds is surfacing underwater mysteries
item.description: 🌊 Exciting developments in marine biology! Google DeepMind’s Perch 2.0, a bioacoustics model initially trained on bird sounds, is now successfully applied to underwater acoustics. This model aids in understanding marine species through sound. Recent discoveries, like the elusive “biotwang” sound from Bryde’s whales, highlight the need for innovative technology in monitoring ocean life. Stay tuned for more breakthroughs in bioacoustics! 🐋🔊 #MarineBiology #Bioacoustics #AI #GoogleDeepMind…

item.title: How AI agents can redefine universal design to increase accessibility
item.description: Google Research introduces Natively Adaptive Interfaces (NAI) to enhance accessibility through multimodal AI tools. This framework aims to transform digital design by making interfaces adaptable to individual user needs, particularly for the 1.3 billion people with disabilities worldwide. By collaborating with disability communities, Google ensures that development reflects real experiences. Learn more about how this initiative can reshape universal design! 🌍🤖 #Accessibility #AI…

item.title: ​Sequential Attention: Making AI models leaner and faster without sacrificing accuracy
item.description: Introducing Sequential Attention, a new algorithm designed to improve the efficiency of large-scale machine learning models. 🧠✨ This approach focuses on feature selection, a crucial yet challenging task in ML. By identifying and retaining the most informative input variables, it effectively reduces redundancy within complex model architectures. Sequential Attention employs a greedy selection mechanism to optimize model components while minimizing training costs. This allows for scalable…

item.title: Collaborating on a nationwide randomized study of AI in real-world virtual care
item.description: 🚀 Exciting news in healthcare! We are partnering with Included Health to launch a nationwide study on conversational AI in virtual care. This research aims to gather evidence on AI performance in real clinical settings, beyond simulations. The study will assess how AI can enhance access to medical expertise and support physicians in patient care. Pending IRB approval, this marks a significant step in our ongoing research journey. Stay tuned for updates! #HealthcareAI #VirtualCare…

item.title: Towards a science of scaling agent systems: When and why agent systems work
item.description: New research explores scaling principles for AI agent systems through a study of 180 configurations. Key findings show that while multi-agent coordination boosts performance in parallel tasks, it can hinder outcomes in sequential ones. A predictive model was also developed to identify optimal architectures for 87% of unseen tasks. This challenges the common belief that “more agents are better,” highlighting the need for careful design in agent systems. #AI #AgentSystems #GenerativeAI…

item.title: ATLAS: Practical scaling laws for multilingual models
item.description: 🚀 ATLAS introduces new scaling laws for multilingual language models, addressing the gap in research focused on non-English languages. Over 50% of AI users communicate in languages other than English, yet existing models predominantly emphasize English. ATLAS aims to change that by providing data-driven guidance for developing multilingual models. The study includes 774 training runs across 10M–8B parameter models and evaluates data from 400+ languages. It presents adaptive transfer scaling…

item.title: Introducing GIST: The next stage in smart sampling
item.description: 🚀 Introducing GIST: a groundbreaking algorithm designed for smart data sampling! GIST offers a solution for selecting high-quality data subsets that maximize both diversity and utility. This is crucial as modern machine learning requires handling vast datasets efficiently. The algorithm balances two key objectives: ensuring selected data points are diverse and useful for training accurate models. GIST has shown superior performance in tasks like image classification, backed by mathematical…

item.title: Small models, big results: Achieving superior intent extraction through decomposition
item.description: Unlocking user intent from UI interactions is crucial for enhancing mobile experiences. 📱 A recent study introduces a method using small multimodal models to effectively predict user actions without relying on large, resource-heavy models. By breaking down intent extraction into two stages, the approach maintains high accuracy while operating on-device, ensuring user privacy. 🔍 This innovative technique shows promise for future AI agents that can better anticipate user needs based on past…

item.title: Unlocking health insights: Estimating advanced walking metrics with smartwatches
item.description: Recent research highlights the effectiveness of smartwatches in estimating gait metrics, such as walking speed and step length. 📊 Traditionally, measuring these metrics required specialized equipment, but smartwatches provide a practical solution for continuous tracking. This study confirms that they can deliver accurate results comparable to smartphones. 🕒 A deep learning model was developed to enhance this capability, using user height and IMU data for precise measurements. #Smartwatches…

item.title: Hard-braking events as indicators of road segment crash risk
item.description: 🚗📊 New research highlights a strong link between hard-braking events (HBEs) and crash rates on road segments. By analyzing data from Android Auto alongside public crash statistics, the study suggests that frequent HBEs can serve as early indicators of potential road safety issues. This approach moves beyond traditional reliance on historical crash data, paving the way for proactive traffic safety assessments. #RoadSafety #TrafficAnalysis #HBEs #DataDriven #SafetyFirst

item.title: Next generation medical image interpretation with MedGemma 1.5 and medical speech to text with MedASR
item.description: 🚀 Exciting updates in healthcare AI! Google is enhancing the MedGemma model to improve medical imaging support. Alongside this, they introduced MedASR, a new open medical speech-to-text model. The adoption of AI in healthcare is happening rapidly, with the industry embracing it at twice the rate of the broader economy. The MedGemma collection has seen millions of downloads and numerous community variants. #HealthcareAI #MedTech #AIInnovation #MedGemma #MedASR

item.title: Dynamic surface codes open new avenues for quantum error correction
item.description: 🌟 New advancements in quantum error correction (QEC) are here! Researchers at Google Quantum AI introduced dynamic circuits that enhance QEC by using fewer couplers and eliminating correlated errors. This innovation is vital for achieving the low error rates needed for effective quantum algorithms. In December 2024, the Willow quantum processor successfully demonstrated error correction below threshold levels, showing improved robustness of logical qubits. These dynamic circuits offer a…

item.title: NeuralGCM harnesses AI to better simulate long-range global precipitation
item.description: 🌧️ NeuralGCM is revolutionizing the simulation of global precipitation by combining physics-based modeling with AI. This hybrid model, trained on NASA precipitation data, shows significant improvements in forecasting daily precipitation cycles and extreme weather events. NeuralGCM outperforms traditional models, offering enhanced accuracy for medium-range forecasts and long-term climate simulations. This advancement supports better decision-making for farmers and city planners alike….

item.title: Google Research 2025: Bolder breakthroughs, bigger impact
item.description: 🚀 In 2025, Google Research made significant strides, enhancing its impact on products, science, and society. Teams focused on both bold innovations and applied research, leading to breakthroughs in AI, quantum computing, and Earth sciences. Their efforts also addressed key societal challenges like climate resilience and health. 📊 Collaboration was key, as many teams joined forces to translate research into real-world solutions. #GoogleResearch #Innovation #AI #QuantumComputing #Science

item.title: Gemini provides automated feedback for theoretical computer scientists at STOC 2026
item.description: 🚀 Exciting advancements in theoretical computer science! A new tool using Gemini AI was tested at the STOC 2026 conference to help authors verify their submissions. It provided automated feedback within 24 hours, identifying calculation and logic errors. The feedback included structured insights on contributions, potential mistakes, and minor corrections, enhancing the quality of research before peer review. #TheoreticalComputerScience #STOC2026 #AI #ResearchInnovation #GeminiAI

item.title: Titans + MIRAS: Helping AI have long-term memory
item.description: 🚀 Exciting advancements in AI! The Titans architecture and MIRAS framework are introduced to enhance AI’s long-term memory while it operates. These innovations allow models to process extensive contexts efficiently, updating their memory in real-time. Unlike traditional methods, Titans combines the speed of RNNs with the accuracy of transformers, enabling AI to adapt dynamically to new information without requiring offline retraining. #AI #MachineLearning #TechInnovation #LongTermMemory…

item.title: From Waveforms to Wisdom: The New Benchmark for Auditory Intelligence
item.description: Introducing the Massive Sound Embedding Benchmark (MSEB) — a new open-source platform designed to measure machine sound intelligence. 🎶 MSEB unifies eight essential capabilities such as transcription, classification, and reconstruction to enhance auditory performance in AI systems. It aims to address key questions about sound-based AI, including the potential for universal sound embeddings. This benchmark provides a structured evaluation framework and sets clear performance goals,…

item.title: Reducing EV range anxiety: How a simple AI model predicts port availability
item.description: 🚗🔌 A new AI model predicts EV charging port availability, helping drivers minimize wait times and plan trips effectively. This solution targets “range anxiety” by integrating charging stations into routes based on battery levels. The model, based on simple linear regression, balances predictive power with efficiency. By using real-time data, it enhances user experience and supports the growing need for reliable EV infrastructure. #ElectricVehicles #AI #ChargingStations #RangeAnxiety…

item.title: Real-time speech-to-speech translation
item.description: Introducing a groundbreaking speech-to-speech translation (S2ST) model that enables real-time communication with just a 2-second delay! 🌍🎤 This innovative system allows for direct audio translation in the original speaker’s voice, enhancing connection across language barriers. It overcomes previous delays and errors associated with traditional methods. The model utilizes a streaming framework and a scalable data acquisition pipeline to support multiple languages, making it suitable for…

item.title: Generative UI: A rich, custom, visual interactive user experience for any prompt
item.description: 🚀 Exciting news in the world of AI! A new implementation of generative UI is now available in the Gemini app and Google Search. This technology allows AI to create immersive and interactive user experiences on the fly, tailored to any prompt. Generative UI can generate web pages, games, and applications based on simple or detailed instructions. Research shows these dynamic interfaces are preferred over traditional static designs. Explore the future of personalized interactions with this…

item.title: Separating natural forests from other tree cover with AI for deforestation-free supply chains
item.description: 🌍 The “Natural Forests of the World 2020” map uses AI to distinguish natural forests from other types of tree cover. This tool is essential for achieving deforestation-free supply chains and protecting ecosystems. Forests play a key role in regulating rainfall, storing carbon, and supporting biodiversity. However, deforestation remains a significant challenge. Current mapping often confuses natural forests with plantations, complicating conservation efforts. New regulations, such as the EU’s…

item.title: A new quantum toolkit for optimization
item.description: 🚀 New insights from Google Quantum AI reveal that large-scale quantum computers may solve complex optimization problems beyond the reach of classical computers. 🔍 This potential spans various applications, from airline route planning to clinical trials. A new algorithm, Decoded Quantum Interferometry (DQI), uses quantum mechanics to find near-optimal solutions efficiently. 🧩 However, it also requires solving a challenging decoding problem, which has been studied extensively due to its…

item.title: Differentially private machine learning at scale with JAX-Privacy
item.description: 🚀 Exciting news in AI! JAX-Privacy 1.0 has been released, a library designed for differentially private machine learning on the JAX platform. This toolkit allows researchers to train deep learning models while ensuring data privacy. JAX, known for its high-performance capabilities, supports seamless scaling and efficient model training. JAX-Privacy enhances these features by providing tools for implementing and auditing private algorithms. This release marks a significant step for researchers…

item.title: Introducing Nested Learning: A new ML paradigm for continual learning
item.description:
Source: Google Research

Read full article

item.title: DS-STAR: A state-of-the-art versatile data science agent
item.description:
Source: Google Research

Read full article

item.title: Exploring a space-based, scalable AI infrastructure system design
item.description: 🚀 Project Suncatcher is a new initiative exploring AI infrastructure in space. The project aims to utilize solar-powered satellite constellations equipped with TPUs and optical links to enhance machine learning capabilities in orbit. Solar energy in space can be significantly more productive than on Earth, potentially revolutionizing AI compute. The early research paper outlines challenges like satellite communication and radiation effects. #SpaceTech #AI #Innovation #ProjectSuncatcher…

item.title: Accelerating the magic cycle of research breakthroughs and real-world applications
item.description: 🚀 Exciting advancements were shared at the recent Research@ event by Google Research, showcasing breakthroughs in earth science, genomics, and quantum computing. These innovations are part of a “magic cycle” where research and real-world applications drive each other forward. Enhanced AI tools and open platforms are accelerating this process, leading to new opportunities in various fields. 🌍🔬 Stay tuned for more updates on how technology is shaping our future! #GoogleResearch #Innovation #AI…

item.title: Toward provably private insights into AI use
item.description: Unlocking the potential of generative AI while ensuring user privacy is crucial. A new system called Provably Private Insights (PPI) uses federated analytics to analyze on-device generative AI data without exposing personal information. It combines large language models and differential privacy to provide insights into user interactions. This approach is already being implemented in Google’s Recorder app, allowing developers to improve AI tools while maintaining transparency in data handling….

item.title: StreetReaderAI: Towards making street view accessible via context-aware multimodal AI
item.description: Introducing StreetReaderAI, a groundbreaking prototype aimed at making street view experiences accessible for everyone. 🌍✨ This innovative tool utilizes context-aware, real-time AI to provide blind and low-vision users with descriptive information about their surroundings. Key features include real-time descriptions of streets and locations, dynamic conversations with a multimodal AI agent, and accessible navigation controls. StreetReaderAI represents a significant step towards inclusivity in…

item.title: How we are building the personal health coach
item.description: 🚀 Exciting news in health tech! A new personal health coach powered by Gemini models is set to provide tailored coaching based on individual health data. This initiative aims to bridge gaps in health and fitness support, offering insights on sleep, fitness, and well-being. Starting tomorrow, eligible Fitbit Premium users in the US can access a public preview. The coach will use behavioral science and personal metrics to help users set goals and build lasting habits. Stay tuned for more…

item.title: Google Earth AI: Unlocking geospatial insights with foundation models and cross-modal reasoning
item.description: 🌍 Google Earth AI is enhancing our understanding of the planet with advanced geospatial AI models and reasoning agents. These innovations provide actionable insights on environmental issues and natural disasters. By integrating various data sources, users can address complex queries like hurricane landfalls and community preparedness. New features include updated imagery models and a geospatial reasoning agent for intricate problem-solving. Explore the potential of these tools today!…

item.title: A verifiable quantum advantage
item.description: 🌟 Exciting developments in quantum computing! A recent study in Nature introduces a new computational task measuring Out-of-Time-Order Correlators (OTOCs), showcasing a verifiable quantum advantage. This breakthrough could help tackle real-world challenges, such as Hamiltonian learning in Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR). The research highlights chaos in both macroscopic and quantum systems, emphasizing how quantum computers can effectively simulate these complex, chaotic behaviors. 🔍…

item.title: A picture's worth a thousand (private) words: Hierarchical generation of coherent synthetic photo albums
item.description: Introducing a new method for generating differentially private synthetic photo albums! 📷✨ This approach uses an intermediate text representation to create albums hierarchically, ensuring individual data privacy while maintaining thematic coherence across images. By leveraging generative AI models, organizations can simplify their data analysis workflows, safeguarding sensitive information without compromising quality. Explore the potential of this innovative technique! #DifferentialPrivacy…

item.title: Teaching Gemini to spot exploding stars with just a few examples
item.description: 🌌 In a recent study published in Nature Astronomy, researchers demonstrate how Google’s Gemini model can effectively classify cosmic events, achieving 93% accuracy with just 15 examples per survey. 🔭 This innovative approach transforms Gemini into an astronomy assistant capable of explaining its reasoning in simple terms, addressing the challenges posed by the increasing volume of data from telescopes. 👨‍🔬 This advancement could significantly aid astronomers in distinguishing real events from…

item.title: Solving virtual machine puzzles: How AI is optimizing cloud computing
item.description: Introducing LAVA, a new AI-driven scheduling algorithm designed to enhance resource efficiency in cloud data centers. ☁️💻 LAVA adapts to the lifetimes of virtual machines (VMs) by continuously updating predictions, addressing the challenges of VM allocation. This system helps prevent resource stranding and optimizes server usage for both economic and environmental benefits. 🌱📈 The approach includes three algorithms: NILAS, LAVA, and LARS, which work together to improve VM fitting on physical…

item.title: Using AI to identify genetic variants in tumors with DeepSomatic
item.description: 🚀 DeepSomatic is an AI tool designed to identify cancer-related mutations in tumor genetic sequences. This innovation assists in understanding the genetic factors driving different types of cancer, which is crucial for developing targeted treatment plans. Developed in collaboration with the University of California, Santa Cruz Genomics Institute, DeepSomatic uses machine learning to enhance the accuracy of variant discovery across various sequencing technologies. Both the tool and its…

item.title: Coral NPU: A full-stack platform for Edge AI
item.description: 🌐 Introducing Coral NPU, a new open-source platform designed to enhance Edge AI on low-power devices and wearables. This full-stack solution addresses key challenges such as performance gaps, fragmentation, and user privacy. By embedding AI directly into personal devices, it aims to deliver assistive experiences while maintaining data security. Developers can now access documentation and tools to start creating efficient, always-on AI applications. #EdgeAI #CoralNPU #GenerativeAI #Privacy…

item.title: XR Blocks: Accelerating AI + XR innovation
item.description: 🚀 Exciting news in the world of AI and XR! XR Blocks is an open-source framework aimed at developing immersive web experiences. It combines AI and extended reality to enhance intelligent computing. While AI has established frameworks, bridging the gap with XR has been challenging. XR Blocks offers a modular architecture for rapid prototyping of AI + XR applications. Discover more with live demos and templates available on GitHub! #AI #XR #Innovation #OpenSource #Technology

item.title: ​​Speech-to-Retrieval (S2R): A new approach to voice search
item.description: Introducing the Speech-to-Retrieval (S2R) engine, a new advancement in voice search technology! 🎤🔍 This innovative approach allows users to get answers directly from their spoken queries, eliminating the need for text conversion. This results in faster and more reliable search results. Traditional voice search relies on automatic speech recognition (ASR), which can misinterpret queries and lead to irrelevant results. S2R aims to improve this by addressing those challenges. #VoiceSearch…

item.title: A collaborative approach to image generation
item.description: Introducing PASTA, a new reinforcement learning agent designed to enhance text-to-image generation by learning user preferences through interactive refinement. 🎨🤖 This innovative approach transforms the process from trial-and-error to a collaborative conversation, making it easier for users to achieve their ideal images. PASTA outperformed traditional models in user satisfaction during evaluations. A dataset of over 7,000 interactions has been released to support this research. 📊✨…

item.title: Introducing interactive on-device segmentation in Snapseed
item.description: 🚀 Exciting news for Snapseed users! The app now features a new Object Brush tool for iOS, enabling real-time image segmentation. This allows for easier and more intuitive selective edits, such as brightening a subject or enhancing the sky. Simply draw on the object you want to adjust without affecting the rest of the image. Try it out as this feature rolls out in the coming week! 📸✨ #Snapseed #PhotoEditing #MobilePhotography #TechUpdate #UserExperience

item.title: AI as a research partner: Advancing theoretical computer science with AlphaEvolve
item.description: Discover how AlphaEvolve, a coding agent powered by LLMs, is advancing theoretical computer science. This innovative tool helps find and verify combinatorial structures that enhance our understanding of optimization problems. The study highlights AlphaEvolve’s iterative process to improve code snippets, resulting in significant findings in complexity theory. Learn more about the potential of AI in mathematical discovery! 🤖📊 #TheoreticalComputerScience #AI #AlphaEvolve #Mathematics #Research

item.title: The anatomy of a personal health agent
item.description: Discover the potential of a new research prototype: the Personal Health Agent (PHA). This LLM-powered tool analyzes data from wearable devices and health metrics to provide tailored health insights and coaching. The PHA uses a multi-agent system to address diverse health inquiries, ensuring personalized support. Extensive evaluations involved data from over 1,200 users, marking a significant advancement in health technology. This framework is intended for research and not a market-ready…

item.title: Towards better health conversations: Research insights on a “wayfinding” AI agent based on Gemini
item.description: A new research article explores a “Wayfinding AI” agent that enhances health conversations. This AI helps users navigate health information by asking clarifying questions, understanding individual goals, and providing tailored responses. User studies revealed challenges in articulating health concerns, indicating a need for more personalized guidance. The findings suggest that proactive engagement from AI can lead to a better user experience. 🤖💬 #HealthTech #AI #UserExperience…

item.title: AfriMed-QA: Benchmarking large language models for global health
item.description: Introducing AfriMed-QA! 🌍 This new initiative focuses on evaluating large language models (LLMs) for health-related questions in Africa. It aims to bridge the gap in medical knowledge and support across diverse contexts. Developed with multiple partners, the dataset includes questions from medical schools in 16 African countries. This effort addresses the need for localized benchmarks in healthcare AI. Find out more about how this could enhance clinical decision support in low-resource…

item.title: Time series foundation models can be few-shot learners
item.description: 🚀 Exciting advancements in time-series forecasting! A new approach allows time-series foundation models to learn from just a few examples, enhancing prediction accuracy without the need for extensive training. This builds on the existing TimesFM model, which previously functioned as a zero-shot learner. The method, highlighted in “In-Context Fine-Tuning for Time-Series Foundation Models,” simplifies the forecasting process, making it more efficient for businesses to adapt to various needs….

item.title: Deep researcher with test-time diffusion
item.description: Introducing Test-Time Diffusion Deep Researcher (TTD-DR), a groundbreaking framework in machine intelligence. 🤖📚 TTD-DR utilizes a Deep Research agent to draft and refine research reports using high-quality information. This method leads to state-of-the-art results in long-form writing and complex reasoning tasks. Unlike traditional DR agents, TTD-DR emulates the iterative human research process, enhancing drafts through research and revision. This innovative approach mirrors the retrieval-…

item.title: Sensible Agent: A framework for unobtrusive interaction with proactive AR agents
item.description: Introducing Sensible Agent, a pioneering framework for proactive AR interaction. 🌟 This research prototype adapts its suggestions based on real-time context like gaze and hand availability, addressing limitations of current AR agents that rely on verbal commands. Sensible Agent aims to provide seamless, unobtrusive assistance in everyday situations, enhancing user experience. Learn more about this innovative approach at UIST 2025! #AugmentedReality #HumanComputerInteraction #TechInnovation…

item.title: Making LLMs more accurate by using all of their layers
item.description: Introducing SLED, a new decoding strategy aimed at improving the accuracy of large language models (LLMs) by utilizing all model layers. This method aligns outputs with intrinsic knowledge, tackling the issue of “hallucination,” where models generate incorrect information. SLED enhances factuality without requiring external data or additional fine-tuning. Learn more about this innovative approach presented at NeurIPS 2024! 📊💡✨ #AI #MachineLearning #LLMs #SLED #NeurIPS2024

item.title: Learn Your Way: Reimagining textbooks with generative AI
item.description: 📚 Exciting developments in education are here! New research on generative AI (GenAI) reveals innovative ways to transform textbooks into personalized learning experiences. Google’s “Learn Your Way,” now in Google Labs, aims to enhance engagement by allowing students to tailor their learning paths with diverse formats. Early studies show that students using this approach scored 11 percentage points higher on retention tests compared to traditional methods. Explore how GenAI can reshape…

item.title: VaultGemma: The world's most capable differentially private LLM
item.description: 🚀 Introducing VaultGemma, the largest differentially private language model trained from scratch! As AI becomes more integrated into daily life, ensuring privacy is essential. VaultGemma uses differential privacy (DP) to protect user data by adding calibrated noise that prevents memorization. Our research with Google DeepMind highlights the trade-offs of applying DP in large language models, including increased computation costs and altered training dynamics. We are excited to release…

item.title: Speculative cascades — A hybrid approach for smarter, faster LLM inference
item.description: Introducing “speculative cascades,” a new method enhancing the efficiency of LLMs by merging speculative decoding with standard cascades. This approach aims to reduce inference costs while maintaining output quality. It utilizes smaller models to handle simpler tasks, reserving larger models for complex queries. By combining these techniques, speculative cascades achieve faster results at lower costs, as demonstrated in tests with Gemma and T5 models. #AI #LLM #MachineLearning #TechInnovation…

item.title: Smarter nucleic acid design with NucleoBench and AdaBeam
item.description: 🚀 Exciting advancements in nucleic acid design! Researchers have developed NucleoBench, an open-source benchmark for evaluating nucleic acid sequence design algorithms. This tool runs over 400,000 experiments across various biological challenges to improve therapeutic development. Alongside NucleoBench, they introduced AdaBeam, a new algorithm that outperforms existing methods on 11 out of 16 tasks, showing better scalability for complex models. Both NucleoBench and AdaBeam are available for…

item.title: Accelerating scientific discovery with AI-powered empirical software
item.description: 🚀 Exciting advancements in scientific research! A new AI system has been developed to assist scientists in writing empirical software, achieving expert-level results across six challenging benchmarks. This system addresses the bottleneck in hypothesis evaluation by generating high-quality custom software. By leveraging large language models, the AI can propose and implement novel concepts, optimizing performance through extensive code iteration. This innovation spans diverse fields including…

item.title: How Google’s AI can help transform health professions education
item.description: Google’s AI is shaping the future of health professions education. 🌍 With a projected shortage of over 11 million healthcare workers by 2030, AI tools are being explored to enhance medical learning. Two studies reveal how Google’s AI models can serve as personalized learning aids for medical students. The first study focused on using generative AI to support clinical reasoning, while the second assessed LearnLM, an AI model tailored for education. Results show strong interest in AI that…

item.title: A scalable framework for evaluating health language models
item.description: Introducing a new framework for evaluating health language models! 🏥 This adaptive methodology improves efficiency and inter-rater reliability, reducing the reliance on costly human experts. By using simple boolean questions, it streamlines the evaluation process for complex health queries. The framework focuses on personalized health data and is validated in the metabolic health domain, covering conditions like diabetes and obesity. Learn more about this innovative approach! 🔍🤖 #HealthTech…

item.title: From massive models to mobile magic: The tech behind YouTube real-time generative AI effects
item.description: YouTube is enhancing user experience on mobile with real-time generative AI effects. 📱✨ By utilizing knowledge distillation and MediaPipe, YouTube has developed a solution to deliver over 20 effects directly on creators’ phones. This process involves creating smaller, efficient models tailored for specific tasks, allowing for seamless video processing. These advancements make features like cartoon style transfer not only possible but also fun and interactive for creators on YouTube Shorts. 🎨🎥…

item.title: Securing private data at scale with differentially private partition selection
item.description: 🔒 New algorithms are advancing user privacy in data sharing through differentially private partition selection. This approach allows for safe sharing of large datasets, crucial for AI and machine learning innovation. It ensures individual contributions remain confidential by adding controlled noise during selection. Parallel algorithms enhance efficiency, enabling the processing of vast datasets while maintaining robust privacy guarantees. Explore more in the publication “Scalable Private…

item.title: Beyond billion-parameter burdens: Unlocking data synthesis with a conditional generator
item.description: Unlocking data synthesis in AI just got easier! 🌐 A new algorithm, CTCL, enables the generation of synthetic data while preserving privacy, using a lightweight 140 million parameter model. This approach avoids the complexities of fine-tuning billion-scale models, making it accessible for resource-constrained applications. CTCL conditions data on topic information, ensuring better topic distribution matching. It also allows for unlimited synthetic data generation without additional privacy…

item.title: Enabling physician-centered oversight for AMIE
item.description: Introducing guardrailed-AMIE (g-AMIE), a new AI diagnostic tool designed for history-taking in healthcare. 🩺 g-AMIE operates under strict guidelines, preventing it from giving personalized medical advice. Instead, it generates summaries for physicians to review, ensuring patient safety and oversight. This system allows primary care physicians to maintain accountability while leveraging AI for efficiency. The recent study demonstrated g-AMIE’s performance being preferred over nurse…

item.title: Achieving 10,000x training data reduction with high-fidelity labels
item.description: A new method in active learning significantly reduces the training data needed for fine-tuning large language models (LLMs). 📉 This innovative approach addresses the challenges in classifying unsafe ad content, which requires deep contextual understanding. Traditional methods are costly and often ineffective with evolving safety policies. The new curation process can cut training data from 100,000 examples to under 500, while improving model alignment with human experts by up to 65%. This is…

item.title: Insulin resistance prediction from wearables and routine blood biomarkers
item.description: New research highlights a promising method for predicting insulin resistance using wearable data and routine blood tests. This approach makes early screening for type 2 diabetes more accessible and scalable. Early detection of insulin resistance is crucial, as timely lifestyle changes can reverse the condition. Traditional testing methods can be invasive and costly, creating barriers for many. The study utilized machine learning models to analyze data from wearables and common blood tests,…

item.title: Highly accurate genome polishing with DeepPolisher: Enhancing the foundation of genomic research
item.description: Introducing DeepPolisher, a new deep learning tool that enhances the accuracy of genome assemblies by correcting base-level errors. 🧬 This advancement plays a crucial role in refining the Human Pangenome Reference, making it easier to study heredity, disease, and evolution. DeepPolisher reduces assembly errors by 50% and indel errors by 70%, improving gene identification significantly. This open-source method was developed in collaboration with UC Santa Cruz Genomics Institute, marking a step…

item.title: MLE-STAR: A state-of-the-art machine learning engineering agent
item.description: Introducing MLE-STAR, a cutting-edge machine learning engineering agent designed to automate various ML tasks across different data types. 📊🤖 This innovative agent streamlines the complex process of developing machine learning models by using large language models (LLMs) to transform task descriptions into executable code. This approach enhances efficiency for machine learning engineers. While MLE-STAR shows great potential, it still faces challenges that impact its overall effectiveness….
